title: dendromyza erecta
intro: Dendromyza erecta, also known as the 'Stickflower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations, and it has been used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments. In herbal medicine, Dendromyza erecta is often made into a tea or infused in oil to harness its medicinal properties.
